query: FAA eVTOL electric vertical takeoff landing major milestone advanced air mobility

The purpose was to test and evaluate the reliability of electric aircraft for critical organ delivery.
The eIPP is a first-of-its-kind program that was outlined in President Trump’s Unleashing Drone Dominance Executive Order.
In March 2026, the Department of Transportation announced it selected eight projects across 26 states to join the eIPP."},{"url":"https://www.transportation.gov/briefing-room/future-aviation-here-trumps-transportation-secretary-sean-p-duffy-and-faa-unveil","title":"THE FUTURE OF AVIATION IS HERE: Trump’s Transportation Secretary Sean P. Duffy and FAA Unveil Eight Selections for Pilot Program Testing Next-Gen Aircraft in America’s Skies | US Department of Transpo","content":"Together, these pilot projects will create one of the largest real-world testing environments for next-generation aircraft in the world.
Regional passenger transportation (including short Takeoff and Landing aircraft)
“The program will provide valuable operational experience that will inform the standards needed to enable safe Advanced Air Mobility operations.
Industry partners will support regional flights connecting Dallas, Austin, San Antonio, and eventually Houston, with air taxi networks expanding from each city to extend regional reach.
A focused project designed to achieve early advances in autonomous operations through an existing partnership with an advanced autonomy developer already operating in the region and coordinating with the FAA."},{"url":"https://www.faa.gov/newsroom/future-aviation-here-trumps-transportation-secretary-sean-p-duffy-and-faa-unveil-eight","title":"THE FUTURE OF AVIATION IS HERE: Trump’s Transportation Secretary Sean P. Duffy and FAA Unveil Eight Selections for Pilot Program Testing Next-Gen Aircraft in America’s Skies | Federal Aviation Adminis","content":"In addition to offering the American people an exciting window into the future of aviation, data from the pilot projects will be used by the FAA to develop new regulations that safely enable this futuristic technology at scale.
“These partnerships will help us better understand how to safely and efficiently integrate these aircraft into the National Airspace System,” said FAA Deputy Administrator Chris Rocheleau.
The Advanced Air Mobility and eVTOL Integration Pilot Program (e-IPP) received more than 30 proposals from across the country."},{"url":"https://evtol.travel/evtol-timeline","title":"eVTOL Timeline 2026: USA-Forward Air Taxi Milestones | eVTOL.Travel | eVTOL.Travel - Future of Urban Air Mobility","content":"August 2021Investment🇺🇸USACompleted
March 2025Investment🇺🇸USACompleted
February 2026Regulation🇺🇸USACompleted
March 2026Commercial🇺🇸USACompleted
BETA Technologies and Surf Air Mobility announce inter-island Hawaiian routes using the all-electric ALIA CX300 — cargo in 2026, passenger service targeted for 2027.
April 2026Certification🇺🇸USACompleted
Joby flies a piloted JFK ↔ Downtown Manhattan demo flight, the first eVTOL public demonstration on a real future commercial NYC route.
Vertical Aerospace targets EASA/CAA certification of the Valo (formerly VX4).
The first US lead cities are New York, Los Angeles, Dallas-Fort Worth, Houston, and Miami — driven by Joby's NYC partnership with Delta, Archer's deal with United Airlines for the LA28 Olympics, and the eIPP demo program in Texas and Florida.
US commercial operations are expected in 2027 following FAA Type Certification, with a major showcase at the LA28 Summer Olympics in 2028."},{"url":"https://www.aerotime.aero/articles/faa-selects-eight-evtol-pilot-projects","title":"FAA selects eight eVTOL pilot projects","content":"DOT, FAA pick eight eVTOL pilot projects ahead of summer 2026 flights
The DOT announced the selections on March 9, 2026, as part of the Advanced Air Mobility and eVTOL Integration Pilot Program, known as eIPP.
The agency said the program will help generate operational data that the FAA can use to develop future regulations for advanced air mobility aircraft."}]}

A **lock** ( LockA locked padlock ) or **https://** means you’ve safely connected to the .gov website. ## Subnav: Aircraft 1. **WASHINGTON** — The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) today announced that BETA Technologies and United Therapeutics Corporation, in partnership with the Pennsylvania Department of Transportation, flight-tested organ medical-transport capabilities from Virginia to Maryland. This is a major milestone in the Department’s work to safely launch Advanced Air Mobility aircraft into America’s skies under the Trump Administration’s Electric Vertical Takeoff and Landing (eVTOL) Integration Pilot Program (eIPP). “The eIPP gives us a real-world environment to safely test and integrate the next generation of aircraft into our airspace system,” **said FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford**. The Pennsylvania Department of Transportation and other eIPP participants are planning additional test flights throughout the rest of the year.","score":0.90330297,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/faa-cites-major-milestone-advanced-air-mobility-chuck-martin-2da3e","title":"FAA Cites Major Milestone in Advanced Air Mobility","content":"The FAA cited last week's mission by air taxi developer Beta Technologies to complete its first electric conventional takeoff and landing flight ...Read more","score":0.77179235,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/groups/3463282763996744/posts/4601518086839867","title":"FAA Announces Major Milestone in eVTOL Technology Use","content":"EVTOL (electric vertical takeoff and landing) aircraft developer AIR announced the completion of its AIR ONE vehicle's first full forward ...Read more","score":0.7539928,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.transportation.gov/briefing-room/future-aviation-here-trumps-transportation-secretary-sean-p-duffy-and-faa-unveil","title":"THE FUTURE OF AVIATION IS HERE: Trump’s Transportation Secretary Sean P. Duffy and FAA Unveil Eight Selections for Pilot Program Testing Next-Gen Aircraft in America’s Skies","content":"Image 1: U.S. flag. An official website of the United States government Here's how you know. A **.gov** website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. # Image 4: U.S. Department of Transportation. Duffy and the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) today announced eight proposals were selected as part of the brand-new Advanced Air Mobility and Electric Vertical Takeoff and Landing (eVTOL) Integration Pilot Program (eIPP). This first-of-its-kind program, which was outlined in President Trump’s Unleashing Drone Dominance Executive Order, is accelerating the safe integration of next-generation Advanced Air Mobility aircraft into the national airspace and ensuring the United States leads the way in aviation innovation. In addition to offering the American people an exciting window into the future of aviation, data from the pilot projects will be used by the FAA to develop new regulations that safely enable this futuristic technology at scale. _“Thanks to President Trump, the future of aviation is here — and it’s going to dramatically improve how people and products move,”_ said **U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean P.","score":0.74807674,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/FOX35Orlando/posts/the-federal-aviation-administration-faa-announced-monday-that-it-selected-eight-/1503185461167184","title":"FAA selects 8 proposals for eVTOL aircraft testing","content":"The initiative focuses on Electric Vertical Takeoff and Landing vehicles, or eVTOLs, which resemble the flying cars of popular imagination.","score":0.53750324,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.75,"request_id":"660f735a-80fa-4a62-ada1-4d7c50c81a24"}
